1. # **Introduction**  {#introduction}

### **The Imperative for Data Quality at FoodMesh Enterprise Group**

FoodMesh Enterprise Group has a complex technology landscape to serve our customer and team member needs, where data flows from numerous touchpoints including suppliers and customers, and encompasses over 100,000 products, the imperative for robust data quality cannot be overstated. This document establishes a comprehensive view for the FoodMesh Enterprise Group Data Quality Standard, built on a structured, Data Quality framework. 

2. # **Purpose**  {#purpose}

This **Data Quality (DQ) standard** is the foundational guardrails for managing data quality effectively across FoodMesh Enterprise Group, directly aligned with the **Group Data Management Policy**. Its primary purpose is to establish the criteria and processes of the Data Quality lifecycle, ensuring that enterprise data is consistent, accurate, complete, reliable, and fit for purpose. This standard underpins our overarching DQ framework, which includes key DQ components, outcomes and solutions aimed at delivering DQ consistently across the Group. These three tiers of our DQ framework, providing a holistic DQ approach, these include:

1. **Define Core Components:** It defines the objectives of key controls for effective DQ Management, which are the fundamental components of our strategy, including clear data ownership, robust governance for Critical Data Elements (CDEs), and adherence to DQ metric thresholds.  
2. **Establish Key Outcomes:** The standard outlines the key processes and standards—such as our DQ Assessment, Issue Management, and Data Certification—that ensure high confidence in data accuracy, timely remediation of breaches, and effective reconciliation controls.  
3. **Leverage Solutions:** It provides guidance on leveraging the practical DQ solutions, including the CDE Identification Template, the Issue management Framework, and ScoreCards, to enable trusted decision-making, enhance operational efficiency, ensure compliance, and foster a data-driven culture. By applying this standard, all business units will have clear rules and expectations for consistent data management and use, ultimately enabling trusted decision-making, improving customer experiences, and fostering collaboration.  
   

3. # **Scope** {#scope}

This Data Quality Standard sets mandatory requirements for high-quality, trusted data. It applies to all FoodMesh Enterprise Group Business Units (BUs) — including Jaffle Shop, Poutine Shop, Waffle Shop, and Bagel Shop — and Ecosystem Businesses. The Standard aligns with the FoodMesh Enterprise Group Data Management Operating Model. The scope is comprehensive, covering both internally generated and externally sourced data, with critical focus on key enterprise assets, including Customer, Financial, Operational, Team Member, and Product Data. The Standard also covers assets like business applications/systems, reports, metrics, and dashboards. It is applicable to all team members regardless of their role, who generate/manage data across data lifecycle. 

4. # **Data Quality Framework** {#data-quality-framework}

**The FoodMesh Enterprise Group Data Quality Framework** provides a structured approach, defining the principles, policies, processes, and technology used to ensure data is consistently accurate, complete, reliable, and fit for purpose. It acts as the blueprint for managing data quality across FoodMesh Enterprise Group. 

The DQ Framework is based on DAMA DMBOK ( Data Management Book of Knowledge), which is an industry standard in building , deploying and driving value from enterprise wide data governance and data quality initiatives. 

![][image1]

### **4.1 Data Quality Methodology & Community** {#4.1-data-quality-methodology-&-community}

### Establishing consistent methods and fostering a community around data quality. The **Data Quality Methodology** (including established standards and ways of working) is continuously refined and disseminated through dedicated forums. The DQ Community, fostered through initiatives like DQ Working Groups and Data Clinics, provides a forum for collaboration, knowledge sharing, and direct support. 

### **4.1.1 Consistent & Structured Approach (Outcome)**

Ensuring a standardised method for DQ management,these forums enable stakeholders to understand new DQ capabilities, frameworks, tools, dashboards, and address their data challenges, ensuring collective uplift in data maturity. 

### **4.1.2 DQ Working Group, DQ clinic, DGC, BU guilds (Solution)** {#4.1.2-dq-working-group,-dq-clinic,-dgc,-bu-guilds-(solution)}

The DQ framework relies on having key forums prescribed in the Group Data governance operating model that provides the required structure aimed at effectively managing and governing data, including issues impacting data and services. 

| Description | Purpose/ | Cadence |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| BU Guilds | Provide a collaborative forum for data owners and stewards to share knowledge, address challenges, and foster best practices across all business units, including collaboration and prioritisation of data quality issues.  Additionally BU Guilds also act as a decision body for handshake requests which emphasises on uplift/need for certification in alignment with usage. | Determined by  BU |
| Data Governance Council  | This is the primary governing body responsible for overseeing the implementation of data quality standards and ensuring alignment with data management Group policy. | Monthly |
| DQ Clinic/DQ working Group | To provide specific comms on DQ capabilities | Case-by-case basis |

### **4.1.3 Standard** {#4.1.3-standard}

| Standard Ref | Description | Responsibility |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| 4.1.2.1 | BU Guilds-  Every BU (e.g., Jaffle Shop, Poutine Shop, Waffle Shop, Bagel Shop) must establish a forum to facilitate  discussion and prioritisation for DQ issues impacting their business.  | Data Management Lead |
| 4.1.2.2 | Data owners should represent their aging/long running/high priority issues in the BU Guilds for collaboration and prioritisation. | Data Owners |
| 4.1.2.3 | DML’s should escalate and report on issues that require support and collaboration across various BU’s( eg: aging issues that require support, issues impacting multiple BU’s). | Data Management Lead |

### **4.2 [Data Quality Measurement/Assessment](#heading=h.jvw2kkelcp5y)** {#4.2-data-quality-measurement/assessment}

Measuring the quality of our data. Standardised methods and processes for evaluating data against defined quality criteria. Clear definitions for aspects like accuracy, completeness, and timeliness, providing criteria to measure data health.  
The clear definition of **Data Quality Dimensions** (e.g., Accuracy, Completeness, Consistency, Timeliness, Validity, Uniqueness) provides the specific criteria by which data health is measured and assessed. Data Owners rely on these dimensions to determine data's "fitness for purpose," while Data Stewards implement rules and processes to ensure data adheres to these dimensional requirements across data flows and systems.

| Dimension | Definition | Metrics / Rules |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| Accuracy | Data must reflect real-world entities or events correctly | % of verified entries and error rate |
| Completeness | All required data fields must be populated appropriately | % of missing values per critical field |
| Consistency | Ensures data is reflected in a uniform and coherent manner across various systems, datasets and periods. | % of matching values across critical fields |
|  |  |  |
| Uniqueness | Each data record must be uniquely identifiable and not duplicated | Duplicate check |
| Integrity | Relationships between data elements must be correctly maintained | Referential integrity check success rate |
| Validity | Data must conform to the required formats, ranges, and business rules. | % of invalid entries |
| Timeliness | Data must be available within the required timeframe | Data is current at the time of use. |
|  |  |  |

### **4.2.1 Identification and Measurement (Outcome)** {#4.2.1-identification-and-measurement-(outcome)}

Identifying and measuring business-critical data to determine its DQ level.

## **4.2.2 [Data Quality Assessment Process](#heading=h.11az7dojpxy) (Solution)** {#4.2.2-data-quality-assessment-process-(solution)}

The Data Quality Assessment process, conducted by Data Owners and Data Stewards, provides a clear snapshot of current data health against defined **quality dimensions** (referenced in 4.2) and Critical Data Elements (CDEs). It enables Data Owners to oversee the completeness, accuracy, timeliness, and consistency of data, while identifying materialised and emerging data risks. These reviews ensure that the scope of data quality monitoring remains current, and that emerging critical data is handled appropriately.

These assessments are performed at two key junctures in a dataset's lifecycle:

* **During the data procurement/ingestion process:** DQA plays a key role in ensuring that data procured through external parties or data entered into the Group's ecosystem is validated as per the expectations setup by data owners. While systematic controls for validations are applied by system owners, regular monitoring and oversight of DQ Assurance is the responsibility of the data owners.   
* **During the consumption/use (usually through certification process),** Data Stewards and Data Owners undertake a regular DQA process to ensure data remains fit for purpose. This can be streamlined through data certification methodology. 

## **4.2.3 CDE Identification Process (Solution)** {#4.2.3-cde-identification-process-(solution)}

The identification and definition of Critical Data Elements (CDEs), along with their associated Business Rules, are paramount. CDE’s are data attributes that relate to successful business outcomes, improves decision making and aligns with regulatory compliance. Data Stewards lead this hands-on effort, documenting and capturing these rules in a dedicated data quality platform, which directly supports the Data Owner's responsibility to ensure data assets are "fit for purpose". This ensures that the most vital data is understood and its quality criteria are explicitly defined. All KPIs(metrics) must have CDE’s defined and meet a minimum threshold of 70%. 

| Solution Ref | Description | Responsibility |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| 4.2.2.1 | All group-wide data assets undergo CDE assessment to ensure CDEs are defined based on risk, opportunity, and impact.  | Data Owner |
| 4.2.2.2 | The CDE identification should take into consideration the current Group Risk areas in addition to Business Unit (BU) risk areas (for example, Jaffle Shop or Bagel Shop). | Data Owner |
| 4.2.2.3 | Data Elements defined as the direct source of Metrics that matter should be a CDE’s.  | Data Owner |

## 

## **4.2.4 Standard** {#4.2.4-standard}

DQ Standard defined the expectation of the DQ Assessment : 

| Standard Ref | Description | Responsibility |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| 4.2.4.1 | DQ Assessment should be performed for all CDE’s. To ensure data is fit for purpose and to maximise trust, the data steward must conduct a quarterly review of all defined DQ rules and their execution. This review must specifically assess quality performance trends to identify systemic issues. | Data Steward |
| 4.2.4.2 | Data entering the Group's systems is assessed for quality before accepted. | System Owner |
| 4.2.4.3 | DQ Assessment framework, including various applicable dimensions should be evaluated for data quality | Data Steward |
| 4.2.2.3 | Data Owners should undertake regular maintenance for existing CDEs to confirm their continued relevance, accuracy, and alignment with business priorities. These maintenance/review period is based on certification level:  Gold : Every 3 months. Silver: Every 6 months. Bronze: Every year. | Data Owner |
| 4.2.2.4 | Data Owners/system owners should undertake yearly review(at the minimum) of DQ Assurance on systems they manage. | Data Owners/System owners |

### **4.3 Data Quality Tools and Capabilities** {#4.3-data-quality-tools-and-capabilities}

### Utilising the right tools for the job. Group Data Management (GDM) is the FoodMesh Enterprise Group centre of excellence, dedicated to enabling and supporting best practice Data Management to drive safe and trusted data assets. To achieve this, GDM has strategically selected a dedicated **data governance platform** for data governance and a dedicated **data quality platform** for data quality operations.

### **4.3.1 Proactive & Continual Measurement (Outcome)** {#4.3.1-proactive-&-continual-measurement-(outcome)}

Proactively and continually measuring DQ through our best-in-class DM technology and DQ Platform. 

## **4.3.2 Data Quality Rules Management (Solution)** {#4.3.2-data-quality-rules-management-(solution)}

**Data Quality Rule Management (DQRM)** involves centrally defining, testing, and deploying the business rules that govern data integrity and standards across the enterprise. The purpose of DQRM is to establish a **single source of truth** for data quality logic, ensuring standards are consistently applied throughout the data lifecycle for high data trust. This capability provides the business benefit of accelerated **analytics readiness** and **reduced manual effort** through the automation of validation tasks. This proactive approach ensures only **high-quality data** reaches downstream applications, driving reliable reporting and predictable business outcomes.

## **4.3.3 Standard** {#4.3.3-standard}

| Standard Ref | Description | Responsibility |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| 4.3.3.1 | DQ Rules should be in place to evaluate each business rule identified by the CDE process.  | Data Steward |
| 4.3.3.2 | BUs should reuse the existing Group DQ Rules where applicable, instead of creating duplicate rules.  | Data Owner |
| 4.3.3.3 | BU’s should define bespoke DQ rules where appropriate that aligns with their business expectations. | Data Steward. |

### **4.4 Data Issue Management** {#4.4-data-issue-management}

**Data Issue Management** is the formal, structured process for **identifying, documenting, tracking, resolving, and communicating** any instance where data fails to meet defined quality standards, business rules or expectations.

Its purpose is to ensure that data anomalies or issues have right visibility, accountability, oversight and progress necessary to identify and rectify the **root cause**, thereby preventing recurrence and continuously improving the overall data landscape.

The Issue Management Process should underpin the core principles endorsed by the Group Data Governance Council. 

![][image2]

### **4.4.1 Data Issue Management (Outcome)** {#4.4.1-data-issue-management-(outcome)}

Data issue management should drive outcomes that reflect the core issue management principles :

* **Transparency :** Promote visibility of issues to the impacted user group and data community. This drives trust among data users.   
* **Accountability :** Establish formal processes to ensure that Data Stewards and Data Owners are clearly assigned to perform issue management(including diagnosis resolution, escalation).    
* **Prioritisation:** Prioritisation should be embedded into the issue management process to ensure high priority incidents are given the urgency necessary to mitigate team and customer impacts. Additionally systemic resolution is to ensure the root cause of issues are addressed.   
* **Collaboration**: Effective Issue management is achieved through active collaboration between the users who logged issues, stewards and owners supporting resolution and data governance leads required to support escalation. Formal/informal processes are required to support collaboration and team work for streamlining issue management.   
* **Escalation:** Well defined escalation channels available through various BU forums and Group forums act as a platform for escalation of issues, which cuts across BU’s, requiring additional resources and support. 

## **4.4.2 Data Quality Assurance Process (Solution)** {#4.4.2-data-quality-assurance-process-(solution)}

Group wide data issue management capability to allow and encourage Group wide visibility of the data issues, consistent prioritisation and escalation channels thus improving trust and reliability of business critical assets. The **Data Issue Management Guide** establishes the process for escalating data issues when resolution is delayed, when SLA thresholds are breached, or when issues present a higher level of business, regulatory or operational risk.

## **4.4.3 Standards** {#4.4.3-standards}

All data issues identified through monitoring or reported by stakeholders must be managed in accordance with the Group's **Data Issue Management Guide**.

| Standard Ref | Description | Responsibility |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| 4.4.3.1 | All data issues shall be logged. | Team member |
| 4.4.3.2 | Data issues are visible to the Group (other data consumers) to avoid duplication and align with the transparency principle. | Data Owner |
| 4.4.3.3 | Data issues should be mapped to data assets to improve visibility of issues impacting an asset. | Data Owner, Data Steward |
| 4.4.3.4 | Active ownership and stewardship must be in place to triage, respond, resolve and escalate data issues. | Data Management Lead |
| 4.4.3.5 | Data Issues once logged must have a clear resolution plan, along with estimated timeframes to resolution. | Data Steward |
| 4.4.3.6 | BU leads must establish and maintain governance structures for effective monitoring, escalation and resolution of data issues. | BU Leads, Group Data Management (GDM) |
| 4.4.3.7 | High risk, long running and cross BU Data Issues must be escalated to DGC to allow  visibility, prioritisation and assistance from various BU Data leaders represented in DGC | BU Leads |

### **4.5 Data Certification** {#4.5-data-certification}

The Data Certification Framework (Foundation, Bronze, Silver, Gold) provides a structured methodology to formally assess and indicate levels of trust in datasets. Data Owners are responsible for ensuring the adoption of this framework on critical data assets. This directly reinforces the objective of providing "safe and trusted data" and ensures stakeholders have confidence in the data they consume.

Data Stewards must demonstrate compliance with the standards and controls set out by each of the levels of certification for Foundation, Bronze, Silver and Gold Certification standards. 

## **4.5.1 Data Certification Process (Outcome)** {#4.5.1-data-certification-process-(outcome)}

The **Data Certification process** ensures that datasets meet a defined level of quality and trustworthiness before being used across the Group. This process extends the criteria for Foundation, Bronze, Silver, and Gold certification, emphasizing pre-requisites to ensure consistency. Certification is granted in a tiered manner, with each level having progressively higher standards for data quality assurance and governance.

## **4.5.1.1 Standard** {#4.5.1.1-standard}

DQ Certification Levels and Requirements

| Standard Ref | Description |
| :---- | :---- |
| 4.5.1.1 | Data certified at foundation level is used for internal, **ad hoc analysis** and is shared within a single team. Given its limited impact on key services, there is a very low risk of it negatively impacts on business decisions and / or outcomes. |
| 4.5.1.2 | Data certified at Bronze level is used for **operational** decision-making and is shared across teams and businesses within FoodMesh Enterprise Group. While the impact of its quality on the intended services is currently limited, there remains a low risk of negative impact on operational decisions. |
| 4.5.1.3 | Data certified at Silver level is used for **strategic** decisions, including by leadership and external suppliers, its quality has a high impact on key services. There is also a very high likelihood that poor data quality will negatively influence strategic decisions. |
| 4.5.1.4 | Data certified at Gold level is used for Group or **external** reporting (e.g., to a regulatory body) or has legislative obligations and its quality is critical to those services, the impact of poor data quality is considered catastrophic. This poses an imminent and significant risk to the Group's team members, customers or business operations. |
| 4.5.1.5 | Data(Datasets) used to derive **Metrics that Matter**, which are the Group’s most important performance indicators, must be certified to **Gold standard** based on the potential risks posed by surfacing incorrect numbers. To find out more about the MTM criteria and framework, refer to the **Metrics that Matter (MTM) framework** documentation.  |

## **4.5.2 De-Certification/Re-Certification Process (Solution)** {#4.5.2-de-certification/re-certification-process-(solution)}

The **Decertification** and **Re-Certification** Standard ensures the ongoing integrity of certified datasets. It mandates continuous assurance activities and compliance with defined re-certification frequencies to confirm sustained data quality. Datasets are subject to Decertification if they consistently breach quality thresholds, fail to resolve aging issues, or lose assigned Data Owners/Stewards. Conversely, Re-Certification requires full remediation of identified issues, along with a comprehensive data quality re-assessment, and confirmation of assigned, trained Data Owners and Data Stewards, ensuring continuous adherence to the certification framework.

## **4.5.2.1 Standard** {#4.5.2.1-standard}

| Standard Ref | Description | Responsibility |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| 4.5.2.1 | The SLAs are defined as part of the decertification/recertification are enforced.  Bronze \- DQ consistently meets \>70% threshold for 3months and no overdue issues \>30 days for Bronze datasets. Dataset must be recertified after 12 months from the date it is Bronze certified to re-evaluate. Silver \- Consistent DQ Threshold \> 85% within 3 months and No overdue issues \>10days for Silver certified Datasets and ensure at least 2 DQ's are met. Dataset must be recertified after 6 months from the date it is Silver certified to re-evaluate. Gold \- DQ consistently meets Threshold\>95% for 3 months at least and no overdue issues \>7 days  and ensure at least 2 DQ's are met. Dataset must be recertified after 3 months from the date it is Gold certified to re-evaluate.  | Data Management Lead, GDM (DQ Specialist) |
| 4.5.2.2 | Close data issues within defined service level agreements (SLAs). | Data Steward, Data Owners and DML’s. |
| 4.5.2.3 | MTM( Defined for Group Executive reporting) should be Gold standard | Data Steward |

### **4.6 DQ Reporting & Insights Capabilities** {#4.6-dq-reporting-&-insights-capabilities}

This core component of our data quality framework is focused on the mechanisms and tools used to report on the state of data quality and gain valuable insights. It encompasses the entire process of collecting, analyzing, and presenting data quality metrics to various stakeholders. The goal is to provide a clear, comprehensive view of data health, allowing teams to understand the current state, identify trends, and pinpoint areas that require attention. This capability is essential for fostering a data-driven culture and ensuring that data is consistently trusted and used effectively for decision-making.

### **4.6.1 Timely and Relevant Insights (Outcome)** {#4.6.1-timely-and-relevant-insights-(outcome)}

Timely and relevant insights are the key outcomes of our reporting capabilities. These include dashboard views into data quality scores and trends across critical datasets. By making this information readily available and easy to understand, we enable business units to increase their data maturity, allowing them to make informed decisions with high confidence. 

## **4.6.2 ScoreCards (Solution)** {#4.6.2-scorecards-(solution)}

ScoreCards are the specific solutions used to deliver on the "Timely and Relevant Insights" and "DQ Reporting & Insights Capabilities" objectives. They are the tangible dashboards and reports that display data quality scores and trends. ScoreCards serve as a single source of truth for a dataset's quality, providing a standardized way to measure and communicate performance. They enable us to track our progress against established thresholds, identify breaches, and monitor the overall health of our data assets. By utilizing ScoreCards, we ensure that data quality is not just a concept but a measurable and actively managed part of our data lifecycle.

## **4.6.3 Standard** {#4.6.3-standard}

| Standard Ref | Description | Responsibility |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| 4.6.2.1 | DQ should be monitored for ensuring that DQ issues and breaches are addressed in a timely manner. | Data Steward |

### 

5. ## Accountabilities {#accountabilities}

| Group Data Management (GDM) | Develops and promotes group Data Management Policy and Standards; Supports development of Group’s data strategy in line with business and market requirements; Drives and promote responsible management of data to mitigate data and relevant privacy risks; Establishes and run Group wide data governance process and forums; Develops and maintain data management practice and methodologies, providing support and advice to the BUs for consistent implementation across the Group; Develops and manage data management performance indicators for regular reporting; Ensures consistency and accuracy by establishing ongoing monitoring and reporting of quality of data within Group Data Governance and Data Quality applications; Implement and support data management tolling allowing execution of Group Data Management practices; Oversees compliance with data management policy and standards; Facilitates training and communication of this standard to relevant stakeholders; Facilitates training of Data Quality tool to relevant stakeholders |
| :---- | :---- |
| Data Owner | Understands criticality and data quality of data used in their business process, models, or reports; Owns transformation rules used for enrichment of data; Owns Data Quality thresholds and rules applied to their data Monitors Data Quality of data used in their business |
| Data Steward | Provides inputs on the data flow to and from their respective asset; Maintains technical information relevant to their system; Responsible for periodic monitoring of system lineage for any changes; Applies data category and classification to technical metadata based on the Group’s framework; Captures detail view of Data Quality controls applied on their system;  Ensure creation, implementation and review of controls related to data; and Ensure that policies, procedures and metrics are in place for maintaining/improving data quality and the creation, capture and maintenance of metadata (e.g. data lineage);   |
| BU Data Management Lead | Responsible for tracking triggers and changes within their respective BUs. Accountable for Identification and validation of  Data Owners and Stewards Responsible for ensuring all Data Owners and Data Stewards in their business units register and complete the onboarding. |

 

 

6. ## Definitions {#definitions}

 

| Business Definition | Defines the essential meaning of the Data Element in a precise and unambiguous manner, without embedding definitions of other data, systems or underlying concepts. |
| :---- | :---- |
| Business Metadata | Provides the business context of Data Elements and enables end users to understand the information that is delivered including: how the Data Element is defined and used in the business context; and origination of the Data Element. |
| Business rule | A statement intended to assert Data Quality expectations and conformance to meet business requirements |
| Critical Data Element | if incorrect or unavailable would result in material impacts (e.g. fines, loss of trust) to regulatory & audit risk, legal & compliance obligations, or to key business decision making. |
| Data Aggregation | Process of combining data from several measurements to express it in a summarised form |
| Data Capture | The point of origination of data into the Group's systems which includes: manual entry of data by a user via user interfaces; manual and automated data feeds; and data from internal as well as external sources. |
| Data Consumer | A Data Consumer uses the data and understands the criticality of the data to their business process models or reports. IN many cases data consumers further enriches data and blends data with other data assets creating new data. |
| Data Consumption | A process of using data for varied purposes, including reporting and analysis. |
| Data Enrichments | Data that has gone through the process of transformation, aggregation or calculation to address specific business requirements. |
| Data Life-cycle | Refers to end-to-end life-cycle of data from the initial point of capture through to disposal. |
| Data Lineage | Visibility over data origin, providing information as to what happens to data (e.g. data enrichment) and where it moves over time. It greatly enhances ability to trace errors back to the root cause and perform analysis with regards to impact assessment. *System Lineage* – captures system and data flows across the Data Life-cycle, key data provisioning points and formats (e.g. file, application, database, schema, table, API calls) and data integration stages between. *Technical Lineage* – Captures metadata at the field level, for source, interim, and target databases / schemas / tables, files, applications, API calls and requests, enrichments / transformation rules and business logic applied across Data Life-cycle supporting the CDE(s), |
| Data Processing | The application of Business Rules and relevant processes to data. It includes: movement extracting and integrations of data; transformation cleansing and enrichment of data; accessing, analysing, modelling, structuring and aggregation of data; and Updates to data including error fixing. |
| Data Sharing | The distribution of data to known end-users and allowing them to access the data. |
| Data Quality (DQ) Dimension | A way of measuring and assessing an aspect of data to provide a view of the DQ |
| Data Issue  | An instance where CRE does not meet established DQ thresholds and rules.   |
| Group Data Management Framework | A group of resources that consists of: Group Data Management Policy; Data Quality Standard; and Data Governance and Life-cycle Standard |
